reasoning: The interest rate differential between the US and Japan creates a natural downward drift in the USD/JPY forward rate (as implied by uncovered interest rate parity). Recent momentum over the past week has been sharply negative, with the spot rate falling from >157 to around 152.8. Several currency analysts are forecasting a continued drop into the mid-140s by March 2026. The FRED tool’s statistical analysis provides a combined estimate of p=0.45 for exceeding the current threshold, factoring in negative linear trend and weak seasonality. Since the drift and momentum both suggest a decrease is more likely than an increase, my baseline prediction is below 0.5. In accordance with the instruction that uncertainty should increase over time (making probabilities closer to 0.5 for more distant dates), I estimate 0.43 for the 7-day horizon (Feb 22) and 0.47 for the 30-day horizon (Mar 17).
